title: Le conditional
summary: Say what would happen: the conditional tense.

teach.note: Add -rea to the infinitive: parlar → parlarea.
haber is the full infinitive of 'to have' (ha is the present tense).

teach.target: Si io haberea tempore, io parlarea plus interlingua.
teach.roman: si io haberea tempore io parlarea plus interlingua
teach.english: If I had time, I would speak more Interlingua.
teach.ipa: sˈiʲ ˈiʲo hˌaberˈea tˈempore ˈiʲo pˌarlarˈea plˈus ˌinterlˈiŋɡwa

teach.english: It would be nice to see you.
teach.note: esserea = would be (from esser); agradabile = pleasant/nice.

grammar.title: The conditional tense
grammar.body:
Add **-rea** to the infinitive for "would": parlar → **parlarea** (would speak), vader → **vaderea** (would go), esser → **esserea** (would be).
One form for every person, like every other Interlingua tense: io parlarea, tu parlarea, nos parlarea.

teach.target: Si io haberea plus moneta, io vaderea al Europa.
teach.roman: si io haberea plus moneta io vaderea al europa
teach.english: If I had more money, I would go to Europe.
teach.ipa: sˈiʲ ˈiʲo hˌaberˈea plˈus monˈeta ˈiʲo vˌaderˈea al eʊrˈopa

teach.target: Tu poterea apprender interlingua in poc menses.
teach.roman: tu poterea apprender interlingua in poc menses
teach.english: You could learn Interlingua in a few months.
teach.ipa: tˈu pˌoterˈea ˌaprendˈer ˌinterlˈiŋɡwa ˈin pˈok mˈenses

canDo: Say what you would do in a hypothetical situation
Talk about what would be nice
